top_categories.workout_context: Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine's hilly pre-alpine terrain with significant forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 78 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
104ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
8,202ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine. Train it until it feels boring.
78 miles on hilly pre-alpine terrain with significant burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
8,202ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on hilly pre-alpine terrain with significant. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Base fitness for Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Low-cadence force work for 8,202ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine’s 78 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride hilly pre-alpine terrain with significant climbing on fully paved roads at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 78 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 78 miles of hilly pre-alpine terrain with significant climbing on fully paved roads in Varese, Lombardy, Italy.
